details:
Two workflow problems observed while landing the first monster archetype migration (row 67, GoobyRace):

1) Main-wide native CI break in resource_unit_tests (pre-existing; not caused by content/config changes).
   The ctest target for_unit_tests.resource_unit_tests fails with "FAIL: trusted in-root plugin path must
   still load and execute": EVERY Python resource plugin fails to load with pybind errors
   ("Unable to convert call argument '1' to Python object") and crafting.py with "Allowed Python resource
   plugin module is not initialized: json". This reddens the native linux (and windows / linux-coverage)
   jobs on effectively every code PR. Evidence: it failed identically on a config-only PR (row 67, whose
   Gooby-hunt/Nouraajd quest GameTests all passed) and on unrelated code PR #1185 (getEffectiveInteractions),
   which was merged to main with linux/windows/coverage red. linux appears non-required (PRs merge on
   mergeable_state=unstable), so the break is being tolerated fleet-wide rather than fixed, which both masks
   real regressions and forces every controller to hand-verify that its own failure is only this pre-existing
   one before merging.

2) Archetype class/race authoring churns hot shared greenfield files, causing repeated merge conflicts and a
   revert. res/config/creature_classes.json and res/config/creature_races.json are created/edited by many
   concurrent authoring rows (players r51-55, monsters r62/65/67/69/72). Landing row 67 hit add/add then
   modify/delete conflicts across three rebases within ~30 min, including a full revert of the WarriorClass
   extraction (#1192 reverted by #1197). The per-file contention plus an in-flight design revert make the
   cluster unstable to integrate against.

Notes for the optimizer:
- Fix the resource-plugin sandbox / pybind binding so resource_unit_tests passes again, restoring a
  meaningful native gate; until then controllers cannot distinguish new native regressions from this noise.
- Serialize the race/class authoring rows (or split creature_classes.json / creature_races.json per family)
  so concurrent migrations do not collide on one file; sequence player-class extraction vs monster role-class
  creation deliberately given the WarriorClass revert.

affectedPRsObserved: #1185 getEffectiveInteractions (merged to main with linux red)
resourceUnitTestsBreak.assertion: trusted in-root plugin path must still load and execute
resourceUnitTestsBreak.symptom: all Python plugins fail: 'Unable to convert call argument 1 to Python object'; crafting.py 'module is not initialized: json'

fingerprint: resource_unit_tests trusted plugin load fails mainwide archetype creature_classes races json churn conflicts
impact: linux is non-required so the plugin-sandbox break is merged through fleet-wide, masking real native regressions and forcing per-PR manual triage; concurrent race/class authoring collides on two shared files, producing repeated merge conflicts and an extraction revert
suggestedImprovement: Repair the Python resource-plugin pybind binding so resource_unit_tests passes and linux is a meaningful gate again; serialize or per-family split creature_classes.json/creature_races.json authoring to avoid concurrent-edit conflicts
summary: resource_unit_tests fails main-wide (all Python plugins fail to load), reddening linux on every code PR; archetype creature_classes/races.json churn causes repeated merge conflicts
